# DataDetector.Match.SemanticDetails.CalendarEvent

**Structure**

The values the framework returns that represent date components of a calendar event.

## Declaration

```swift
struct CalendarEvent
```

## Discussion

You can use these properties to populate an Event. The event may contain just a single date, such as a start date, but can contain both a start and end date.

- [doc://com.apple.datadetection/documentation/DataDetection/DataDetector/Match/SemanticDetails/CalendarEvent/allDay] is `true` if the date or date range doesn’t specify a time.
- [doc://com.apple.datadetection/documentation/DataDetection/DataDetector/Match/SemanticDetails/CalendarEvent/startDate] may contain the start date of the event.
- [doc://com.apple.datadetection/documentation/DataDetection/DataDetector/Match/SemanticDetails/CalendarEvent/endDate] may contain the end date of the event.
- [doc://com.apple.datadetection/documentation/DataDetection/DataDetector/Match/SemanticDetails/CalendarEvent/endTimeZone] may contain the time zone of the end date of the event.
- [doc://com.apple.datadetection/documentation/DataDetection/DataDetector/Match/SemanticDetails/CalendarEvent/startTimeZone] may contain the time zone of the start date of the event.

## Calendar event properties

- **allDay**: A Boolean value that indicates whether the event is an all-day event.
- **startDate**: An optional date that may contain the start date of the event.
- **endDate**: An optional date that may contain the end date of the event.
- **startTimeZone**: An optional time zone object that may contain the time zone of the start date of the event.
- **endTimeZone**: An optional time zone object that may contain the time zone of the end date of the event.

## Conforms To

- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
